signals.headline: HSBC Confirms Accelerated XRP Adoption | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
HSBC Confirms Accelerated XRP Adoption | YES | HIGH confidence**  
HSBC confirmed on April 15, 2026, that Ripple XRP adoption is accelerating faster than predicted, marking a major turning point for institutional adoption and XRP price potential.[1]

signals.headline: SEC Clarifies Crypto Rules at Roundtable | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
SEC Clarifies Crypto Rules at Roundtable | YES | MED confidence**  
On April 15, 2026, the SEC issued guidance on crypto interfaces, potentially benefiting XRP Ledger's native DEX amid regulatory progress.[2]

signals.headline: Ripple Treasury Launches Native XRP Management | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Ripple Treasury Launches Native XRP Management | NO | HIGH confidence**  
Ripple Treasury enabled CFOs to manage XRP alongside cash on April 1, 2026, integrating it into a $13T payment platform—but this is not breaking news today.[5]

signals.headline: Bank of Korea Policy Hold Expected | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Bank of Korea Policy Hold Expected | YES | HIGH confidence**

The Bank of Korea is expected to maintain its policy rate at 2.5% due to relatively contained price pressures, despite rising energy costs.[1] This expectation was set before the current date.

signals.headline: South Korea Inflation Pressures Building | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
South Korea Inflation Pressures Building | YES | HIGH confidence**

South Korea's March 2026 inflation rose to 2.2% year-on-year, driven primarily by rising global oil prices and currency impacts.[1] However, this data is from March and was analyzed in early April, not breaking news as of today.
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Russia Defends Iran's Nuclear Rights as US-Iran Talks Loom** | YES | HIGH confidence[1][2]
signals.raw:
Russia Defends Iran's Nuclear Rights as US-Iran Talks Loom** | YES | HIGH confidence[1][2]

Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov on April 15 defended Iran's "inalienable right" to uranium enrichment for civilian use, rejecting US and Israeli claims of weaponization and citing no evidence from international inspectors[1]. signals.headline: SEC Meeting Today on XRP Lawsuit Resolution | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
SEC Meeting Today on XRP Lawsuit Resolution | YES | MED confidence**  
An SEC closed-door meeting on April 16, 2026, could end the XRP lawsuit, with Ripple dropping its appeal and fine reduced to $50M per prior statements; community expects high probability of announcement.[4]
